attacking_style: Opportunistic rider who capitalizes on repeated accelerations to shell competitors, timing decisive moves late in reduced sprint situations (3-up). Exploits moments when GC favorites are racing each other rather than launching solo efforts.
team_tactics: Operates with autonomy in breakaways, relying on individual race-reading rather than heavy domestique support. Positioned to exploit tactical gaps when team leaders focused elsewhere.
terrain_preference: Medium mountain stages where repeated accelerations fragment groups, creating small sprint situations that favor his combination of climbing durability and finishing speed over pure climbers.
vulnerability: Lacks explosiveness for single decisive attacks; requires attrition through multiple accelerations to eliminate pure climbers. Dependent on tactical scenarios rather than raw power.
key_patterns: Wins from breakaways after repeated accelerations shell pure climbers like Pozzovivo (2 observations)
Demonstrates tactical intelligence reading race dynamics and competitor weaknesses (2 observations)
Targets alternative GC time-gaining opportunities like intermediate bonuses when viable (2 observations)
Excels in reduced bunch sprints after selection, timing final move precisely (2 observations)
form_trajectory: unknown: observations span 3-year gap (2018-2021) without sufficient recent data to assess current trajectory
